title: I Know Dada
about: I Know Dada, also known as I Know Dadda or Baby, That's My Brother, Like I Know Dada and He Would Never Do Something Like That, refers to a catchphrase and viral video from TikTok in which a Black man, TikToker Stevocamp, says the aforementioned line in a POV video skit titled, "Girls protecting men with SA allegations." Posted in early October 2025, the I Know Dada video sparked several lip dub and video caption memes using the audio and video in the following weeks, commonly paired with jokes about being naively confident about understanding someone or something better than an outsider.
origin:
On October 9th, 2025, TikToker[1] @stevocamp shared a POV video skit, titled "Girls protecting men with SA allegations." In it, he plays a female character, saying, "Baby, that's my brother, like I know Dada, and he would never do something like that." Over one month, the video received over 5.5 million plays and 1.1 million likes.

Also on October 9th, @stevocamp shared the video to his Instagram,[8] @stevoclub, as a Reel, gaining over 229,200 likes over the same timeframe.
